---
title: "Scenario: Proteomics & Mass Spectrometry"
description: >
  Version-controlled LC-MS/MS proteomics workflow with Spectra and QFeatures integration
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

Proteomics and mass spectrometry workflows involve complex data transformations: raw spectra processing, peptide identification, protein quantification, normalization, and statistical analysis. OmicsLake captures these stages with full lineage.

## Setup

```r
library(OmicsLake)
lake <- Lake$new("proteomics_project")
```

## Step 1: Import PSM-level Data

```r
# Peptide-spectrum matches from search engine output
psm_data <- data.frame(
  spectrum_id = paste0("spec_", 1:50000),
  peptide = sample(paste0("PEPTIDE", 1:5000), 50000, replace = TRUE),
  protein = sample(paste0("PROT_", 1:2000), 50000, replace = TRUE),
  score = runif(50000, 0, 100),
  qvalue = runif(50000, 0, 0.1),
  intensity = rlnorm(50000, 15, 2),
  sample = sample(paste0("S", 1:6), 50000, replace = TRUE)
)

lake$put("psm_raw", psm_data)
lake$snap("01_imported")
```

## Step 2: FDR Filtering

```r
lake$ref("psm_raw") |>
  dplyr::filter(qvalue < 0.01) |>
  save_as("psm_filtered", lake)

lake$snap("02_fdr_filtered")
```

## Step 3: Peptide-level Aggregation

```r
lake$ref("psm_filtered") |>
  dplyr::group_by(peptide, protein, sample) |>
  dplyr::summarize(
    intensity = median(intensity),
    n_psms = dplyr::n(),
    best_score = max(score),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  save_as("peptide_quant", lake)

lake$snap("03_peptide_level")
```

## Step 4: Protein-level Rollup

```r
lake$ref("peptide_quant") |>
  dplyr::group_by(protein, sample) |>
  dplyr::summarize(
    intensity = sum(intensity),
    n_peptides = dplyr::n_distinct(peptide),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(n_peptides >= 2) |>  # At least 2 unique peptides
  save_as("protein_quant", lake)

lake$snap("04_protein_level")
```

## Step 5: Normalization

```r
# Median normalization
protein_wide <- lake$get("protein_quant") |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = sample, values_from = intensity)

medians <- apply(protein_wide[, -c(1,2)], 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)
global_median <- median(medians)
norm_factors <- global_median / medians

normalized <- protein_wide
for (s in names(norm_factors)) {
  normalized[[s]] <- normalized[[s]] * norm_factors[s]
}

lake$put("protein_normalized", normalized,
         depends_on = "protein_quant")
ol_commit(note = "Median normalization applied")
lake$snap("05_normalized")
```

## Step 6: Differential Abundance

```r
# Simple t-test per protein (use limma in practice)
sample_groups <- data.frame(
  sample = paste0("S", 1:6),
  group = c("control", "control", "control", "treated", "treated", "treated")
)

lake$put("sample_groups", sample_groups)

# Perform statistical testing
protein_data <- lake$get("protein_normalized")
results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nrow(protein_data), function(i) {
  ctrl <- as.numeric(protein_data[i, c("S1", "S2", "S3")])
  trt <- as.numeric(protein_data[i, c("S4", "S5", "S6")])
  tt <- t.test(trt, ctrl)
  data.frame(
    protein = protein_data$protein[i],
    log2fc = log2(mean(trt) / mean(ctrl)),
    pvalue = tt$p.value
  )
}))
results$padj <- p.adjust(results$pvalue, method = "BH")

lake$put("da_results", results,
         depends_on = c("protein_normalized", "sample_groups"))
lake$snap("06_da_complete")
```

## Step 7: Significant Proteins

```r
lake$ref("da_results") |>
  dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, abs(log2fc) > 1) |>
  save_as("significant_proteins", lake)

lake$snap("07_final")
```

## Using Spectra Objects (Optional)

If working with raw MS data via the `Spectra` package:

```r
library(Spectra)

# Store spectra object
lake$put("raw_spectra", sps)     # Stored as versioned object
lake$snap("spectra_raw")

# After re-calibration
lake$put("calibrated_spectra", sps_calibrated,
         depends_on = "raw_spectra")
lake$snap("spectra_calibrated")
```

## Using QFeatures (Optional)

For hierarchical quantitative data:

```r
library(QFeatures)

# Store the entire QFeatures object
lake$put("qf_experiment", qf)
lake$snap("qfeatures_stored")

# After normalization
qf_norm <- QFeatures::normalize(qf, method = "center.median")
lake$put("qf_normalized", qf_norm, depends_on = "qf_experiment")
lake$snap("qfeatures_normalized")
```

## Full Lineage

```r
lake$tree("significant_proteins")
# significant_proteins
#   └── da_results
#         ├── protein_normalized
#         │     └── protein_quant
#         │           └── peptide_quant
#         │                 └── psm_filtered
#         │                       └── psm_raw
#         └── sample_groups
```

Every protein in the significant list can be traced back through the entire quantification hierarchy.
